sample.title: Why South Korea’s AI Stock Mania Is a Warning to the World
sample.description:
South Korea’s stock market has surged about 200% year-on-year, powered by retail investors chasing an artificial intelligence-fueled chip rally. But with extreme volatility and a heavy reliance on two companies, is this a sustainable boom or a bubble waiting to burst? 

For more on what's happening in stocks, bonds, currencies and commodities and what will move them next, subscribe here to Bloomberg Markets Daily.
https://www.bloomberg.com/account/newsletters/markets-daily

sample.title: This New Medicare Pilot Program Offers GLP-1s For $50 A Month. Here’s What You Need To Know
sample.description:
Americans on Medicare can now purchase GLP-1s for just $50 a month thanks to a temporary pilot program called Bridge.  Patients who meet certain requirements, like having  BMI of over 35 or having obesity-related conditions, may not qualify. CNBC’s Annika Constantino has more.

Reporting by: Annika Constantino

sample.title: ICE Could Sign A $220M Contract With A Taser Maker That Trump Invested Millions In
sample.description:
In February 2026, President Trump invested up to $5 million in top U.S. taser-maker Axon Enterprise. Two weeks later, ICE announced that it was seeking a $220 million contract to more than quadruple its inventory. Experts say that Axon is the only company able to meet the requirement outlined by ICE. CNBC’s Luke Fountain breaks it down.

sample.title: Inside The Rise And Fall Of Kohl's
sample.description:

sample.title: Why Analysts Say The Auto Industry Is Heading For Demographic Cliff
sample.description:
The auto industry is staring at a 'perfect storm' of slowing population growth, longer lasting cars, high prices, and new technology. These factors stand to put significant downward pressure on new car sales by 2040, according to analysts at Bain & Co.. The landscape could be far more competitive and prone to consolidation.

sample.title: How The AI Boom Is Causing A Surge In Demand For Gas Turbines
sample.description:
CNBC's Seema Mody got an exclusive look inside GE Vernova's largest gas turbine plant in Greenville, South Carolina offer fresh evidence that the AI boom is only getting started. Inside the factory, engineers are working side-by-side factory workers to speed up production of this complex machine. Two hundred workers were hired last year and three hundred more are expected to start working at this factory by end of year. Prices of gas turbines are surging more than 300% since 2023 as companies like XAI, Microsoft and OpenAI deploy turbines at their data centers powering AI.

Reporter: Seema Mody
Produced by: Jeffrey Kopp, Noah Broder
Edited by: Darren Geeter
Camera: Shawn Baldwin, David Soltis, Macklin Fishman
Senior Director of Video: Jeniece Pettitt

sample.title: Cleveland Fed President Hammack sees AI fueling inflation, says rate hikes may be necessary
sample.description: Cleveland Federal Reserve President Beth Hammack said Tuesday that “insatiable” demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure is fueling inflation.

sample.title: Waymo and Uber end robotaxi pilot in Phoenix
sample.description: The Uber-Waymo robotaxi pilot program in Phoenix, Arizona, has ended. CNBC's MacKenzie Sigalos has the details.

sample.title: ETF Edge on how SpaceX may be setting a new precedent for other incoming mega IPOs
sample.description: SpaceX is joining the highly tracked Nasdaq 100 Index next week. It’s already part of the Russell 1000 Index. That means more ETFs have to buy the stock to stay aligned with the indexes, and is increasing investors’ exposure to the company . How is this changing the ETF space? And what does it mean for other big IPOS in the pipeline, like Anthropic and Open AI? FTSE Russell head of equity and multi-asset indices Arne Noack and Kathmere Capital CIO Nick Ryder join Dominic Chu on “ETF Edge” to dive in to this and much more.

sample.title: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey on inflation in the UK
sample.description: Andrew Bailey, Bank of England governor, joined CNBC's "Squawk on the Street" to discuss why he is comfortable leaving rates where they are.

sample.title: Argentina superfan lives in the Czech countryside
sample.description:

sample.title: US envoys will not meet Iranian officials, Qatar says | BBC News
sample.description:
US special envoy Steve Witkoff and President Donald Trump's son-in-law ‌Jared Kushner are in Doha to discuss the US-Iran negotiations with mediators, Qatar's foreign ministry spokesman says. 
 
But they will not meet Iranian officials there.
 
In this episode of The Iran War Today, we take a look at rising tensions and diplomatic efforts in the Middle East, with reports and analysis from our correspondents across the region and beyond.
 
With reporting from Bernd Debusmann, Kasra Naji, Carrie Davis, Lina Sinjab, and analysis from Jeremy Bowen.

What does it cost to keep the UK safe? | BBC Newscast
sample.description:
Keir Starmer has unveiled the long awaited defence investment plan which promises an extra £15 billion to help equip the armed forces for the future.   

Despite being one of the difficult issues which ultimately helped bring about his resignation, the prime minister seems to hope the plan will form a part of his legacy.  

But what will it fund and where has the money come from? Alex, James, Chris and security correspondent Frank Gardner discuss.  


00:00 Intro
00:55 The UK's defence investment plan
05:03 What is the £15bn being spent on?
07:47 What is the hybrid navy?
10:41 Is the plan fully funded?
13:02 What are the trade-offs?
15:19 Does this plan actually work?
22:05 Trump, NATO and pressure on the UK to spend more
28:34 Russia's threat to Britain's undersea cables
33:23 What's next for Keir Starmer after Downing Street?


Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news
